channel.title: Cariboo Signals
channel.copyright: © 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.
channel.itunes:subtitle: Technology and society in rural BC with Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:author: Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:summary: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations. Riley brings tech systems thinking with rural roots, while Casey focuses on community development. New episodes every day with weekly themes.
channel.description: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations.

item.title: Wild Spaces And Outdoor Life
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Fishing photos needed for 2026 Gateway fishing guide and [ScienceAlert] Something Else Used to Drive Climate Changes, Ancient Ice Cores Reveal, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life Topics include: satellite, AI, infrastructure, maintenance, funding.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Fishing photos needed for 2026 Gatew...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Something Else Used to Drive Climate Changes,...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Spill containment and cleanup underw...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] Why March Weather Is Weird: Snow, Tornadoe...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] South Cariboo woman helps document 2...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] Your first NAS doesn't need to be expensive...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] UBC study shows artificial turf fiel...

Additional sources provided:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] More rain, high streamflows in the f...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Bringing the heat: 7 temperature rec...
  • The Narwhal: [The Narwhal] Why B.C. is flooding — again
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] How To Bring a Bird’s Song Back from the E...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Barriere to use vacant lot to show F...
  • Gizmodo: [Gizmodo] Scientists Built Tiny, Portable Atomic Clocks—and ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Firefighters tackle blaze at Hungry ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] PEP Air volunteers dedicate time so ...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Mining Created This BC Town. Now It Opposes a New...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Public Transit Expansion Plans Shelved Across BC
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake advocate earns provinc...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Why Pakistani farmers are suing two Ger...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Nvidia's Earth-2 models, including 'climate in a...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. riders soar to podium in Revels...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Indigenous Lands And Innovation
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [TechRadar] Only 9% of global firms are ready to handle AI-driven threats, and many are dangerously overconfident and [TechCrunch] Patreon CEO calls AI companies’ fair use argument ‘bogus,’ says creators should be paid, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Indigenous Lands & Innovation Topics include: broadband, fiber, connectivity, telemedicine, renewable energy.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Only 9% of global firms are ready to handle AI-d...
  • TechCrunch: [TechCrunch] Patreon CEO calls AI companies’ fair use argume...
  • Neowin: [Neowin] Report: AI Overviews are crushing small publishers,...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Cannabis Compounds May Reverse Fatty Liver Di...
  • Yellowhead Institute: [Yellowhead Institute] The New Impact Assessment Regime: Coo...

Additional sources provided:

  • The Guardian Global Development: [The Guardian Global Development] The Myanmar nurses dodging...
  • Nautilus: [Nautilus] Did Music Give Rise to Language?
  • TechCrunch: [TechCrunch] FBI is buying location data to track US citizen...
  • ZDNet: [ZDNet] The hidden metric that makes these portable power st...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] NASA’s Hubble Telescope Spots Comet K1 Exp...
  • Boing Boing: [Boing Boing] Earleaf: an offline audiobook app built out of...
  • Kottke.org: [Kottke.org] “Birds in the United States are not only ...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] Farmer Mac CEO Brad Nordholm discusses state of U.S...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Google developers find that with AI, judg...
  • Toms Guide: [Toms Guide] More than 220 million iPhones under attack from...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] Visual Studio Code's latest update is a big de...
  • Engadget: [Engadget] Hubble catches rare view of a comet crumbling
  • IndigiNews: [IndigiNews] Tla’amin set to reclaim forest stewardship with...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Central Cariboo Islamic Center eyes ...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Community Tech And Governance
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Community Tech & Governance.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Trans Mountain begins assessment work in North Thompson and [Lifehacker] You Can Turn an Old Android Phone Into a Wifi Extender for Your Home Network, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Community Tech & Governance Topics include: broadband, satellite, connectivity, AI, infrastructure.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Trans Mountain begins assessment wor...
  • Lifehacker: [Lifehacker] You Can Turn an Old Android Phone Into a Wifi E...

  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] Linux phones could get even better with postma...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] The Army's $87 million deal with Anduril ...

item.title: Working Lands And Industry
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] 2,400 apply for 200-250 firefighter jobs as B.C. gears up for wildfire season and [Nautilus] Babies Learn the Art of Deception Before Their First Birthday, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Working Lands & Industry Topics include: satellite, AI.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] 2,400 apply for 200-250 firefighter ...
  • Nautilus: [Nautilus] Babies Learn the Art of Deception Before Their Fi...
  • Nautilus: [Nautilus] How Beer Brewing Revolutionized Modern Statistics
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] March Hare Madness in Williams Lake ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] VIDEO: Meteor confirmed in Lower Mai...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Tracking Hospital Overdoses Isn’t Easy as It Seem...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Harvesting Burned Trees May Seem a No-Brainer. Bu...

Additional sources provided:

  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Vaccine Cuts Risk of Common Cancer For Decade...
  • InsideEVs: [InsideEVs] Watch Donut Lab's Mysterious Solid-State Battery...
  • Gizmodo: [Gizmodo] Archaeologists Unearth 43,000 Ancient Egyptian Rec...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] Spaceflight Started 100 Years Ago in a Mas...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Yellowhead Highway under snowfall wa...
  • The Guardian Global Development: [The Guardian Global Development] A photo of Iran’s bombed s...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Birdwatching May Help Protect Your Brain From...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Recycled Plastic Compression Molding With 3D-Prin...
  • CNET: [CNET] Nvidia Wants to Make It Easier to Create an OpenClaw ...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Are we sure that's wise? Minisforum says its new...
  • CNET: [CNET] Nvidia Is Building a Computer for AI Data Centers in ...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Nvidia expects to sell $1 trillion in AI ...
  • Seeded Content: $1M parcel of land boosts cattle farming research on the Pra...
  • The Narwhal: [The Narwhal] $1M parcel of land expands horizons for cattle...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt

item.title: Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [NYT Top Stories] ‘Nobody Owns Us’: How Plans for a Google Data Center Divided an Oklahoma Town and [Williams Lake Tribune] Boulder hits semi driver on Highway 1 near Revelstoke, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling Topics include: broadband, AI, infrastructure, community ownership, funding.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] ‘Nobody Owns Us’: How Plans for a Google D...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Boulder hits semi driver on Highway ...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Province investigates Kwantlen St...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Sudden Sight Loss Risk Almost 5x Higher With ...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] A used RTX 3090 is still the best GPU for l...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] This is probably the most powerful external GPU ...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] CARIBOO OUTDOORS: A brief discussion o...

Additional sources provided:

  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Study Reveals a 'Turning Point' in US Life Ex...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] This 3D-printed privacy-first security came...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Building a Rad Bluetooth Speaker that Didn’t Real...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Each Stressful Person in Your Life May Age Yo...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Major Parkinson's Study Reveals Symptom Diffe...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I turned a Steam Deck into my living room h...
  • Our World in Data: [Our World in Data] Why cheap waste management is key to sto...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] These 3D prints will make Easter gift givin...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I added a $25 HBA card to my home server an...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] The active NVMe cooling trap: Why SSD fans are...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] In Ski Towns, a Bad Snow Year Is Worsening...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] Blackwell's path tracing is 10,000x better ...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt

© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Resilient Rural Futures
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Resilient Rural Futures.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Hackaday] This Printed Zipper Repair Requires No Unsewing and [ScienceAlert] Weight-Loss Drugs Could Cost Just $3 a Month to Make as Patents End, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Resilient Rural Futures Topics include: broadband, telemedicine, renewable energy, solar, AI.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] This Printed Zipper Repair Requires No Unsewing
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Weight-Loss Drugs Could Cost Just $3 a Month ...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] A $7.5 billion cop-tech darling runs head...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Could this be the key to eternal storage? Expert...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] The internet nearly died (and nobody knew)
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Infant from Fort St. John spends ...

Additional sources provided:

  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Building a Neatly Framed Word Clock
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] GNU Radio Gets a Makeover With PimpMyGRC
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] Stop treating your UPS like a dumb battery: 4 ...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Curiosity Cracked Open a Rock on Mars And Rev...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake 97th birthday of incor...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. mountaineer looks to conquer Se...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] The Moon is Safe, For Now: No Collision in 2032 A...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] AI agents are upending the company org ch...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Reverse-Engineering the Bluetooth Fichero Thermal...
  • Toms Guide: [Toms Guide] I can text Claude like a personal assistant now...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] I'm a 78-year-old retiree who's vibe codi...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Is that a Mac mini in disguise? No, but the new ...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Rezoning approved for building 60...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] These 6 Windows background processes might be ...

item.title: Cariboo Voices And Local News
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Cariboo Voices & Local News.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Hundreds of troops train west of Williams Lake in Cougar Fortress exercise and [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake students race duct-tape, cardboard canoes in school challenge, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Cariboo Voices & Local News Topics include: broadband, fiber, satellite, connectivity, telemedicine.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Hundreds of troops train west of Wil...

  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] 150 people died in B.C. in Januar...

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Rezoning approved for building 60-si...

  • Lifehacker: [Lifehacker] How to Spot 'Living Off the Land' Computer Atta...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] PC building will survive the AI crisis, but...

item.title: Wild Spaces And Outdoor Life
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Global News] Fundraiser launched for tech to keep invasive mussels from entering B.C. waters and [TechRadar] 'We have this power from the wind. We have free cooling': This startup wants to build underwater data centers inside wind turbines at sea, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life Topics include: satellite, renewable energy, solar, AI, infrastructure.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

  • Global News: [Global News] Fundraiser launched for tech to keep invasive ...

item.title: Indigenous Lands And Innovation
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Dinner night at Miocene supports Chelsea Lewis Memorial Award for women in the trades and [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] New partnership allows B.C. patients to access surgeries in Alberta, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Indigenous Lands & Innovation Topics include: satellite, precision agriculture, renewable energy, AI, infrastructure.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Dinner night at Miocene supports Che...

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Similkameen First Nations denounce M...

  • IndigiNews: [IndigiNews] As climate, industry warm Okanagan waterways, e...

item.title: Community Tech And Governance
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Community Tech & Governance.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [TechRadar] 'Big tech pays for power — why not broadband?': Hyperscalers signed the rate protection pledge for electricity infrastructure, so should they do it for broadband? An expert chimes in and [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. allowing Vancouver and Victoria to create independent police academies, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Community Tech & Governance Topics include: broadband, data sovereignty, AI, infrastructure, community ownership.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] 'Big tech pays for power — why not broadband?': ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. allowing Vancouver and Victoria...
  • NYT Business: [NYT Business] YouTube Adds Tool to Help Public Figures Repo...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Dutch MPs call for free government-backed VPN, a...
  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] CNC Fort St James campus to close its doors...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] New detection system in Chilliwac...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] New mental health crisis helpline...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] One Key Protein Could Be a Powerful New Targe...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] We Finally Know How Bumblebee Queens Can Surv...
  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] Williams Lake city councillor to propose “a...

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Rally at Surrey border park to mark ...

  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] 'AI has reinvented computing and is driving the ...
